title: festuca ovina var. halleri
intro: Festuca ovina var. halleri, also known as Haller's fescue, is a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The health benefits of Haller's fescue include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
